completedWork: SCR Controlled Rectifier Simulation - NEW: Interactive Silicon Controlled Rectifier (SCR) half-wave controlled rectifier simulation. Teaches power electronics fundamentals with phase-angle control (firing angle α: 0-180°). Features real-time waveform visualization showing input AC, controlled DC output, and gate trigger pulses on canvas. Interactive circuit diagram with animated current flow during conduction, SCR symbol (anode, cathode, gate), and visual firing angle indicator. Adjustable parameters: peak voltage (10-325V), frequency (10-60Hz), firing angle, load resistance. Live calculations: V_avg = (V_peak/2π)×(1+cos(α)), V_rms, output current, power, and power control percentage. Conduction status indicator (CONDUCTING/BLOCKING) with color coding. Quick presets: full power (0°), half power (90°), low power (120°), dim (150°). Animation controls: play/pause, speed adjustment. Educational content covers SCR operation (forward blocking, gate trigger, latching, commutation), firing angle control principles, key formulas, applications (motor speed control, light dimmers, battery chargers, welding, HVAC, power supplies), and pro tips for SCR design.

Inductor Core Design Calculator - NEW: Professional inductor design tool for ferrite core inductors. Core material selection: Ferrite 3C90 (general purpose), N87 (high frequency), 77 (low loss), powder iron (high saturation), molybdenum permalloy. Core shape options: toroid, E-core, pot core, rod. Standard core sizes: TN10-TN50 with AL values, dimensions, saturation flux density. Calculates required turns N = √(L×1000/AL), actual inductance, recommended wire gauge (AWG 28-10) based on current capacity, total wire length, DC resistance, power loss, peak flux density B_max for saturation check, and core loss using Steinmetz equation. Saturation status indicator: OK (green), WARNING (yellow, <20% margin), SATURATED (red, exceeds B_sat). Interactive core visualization showing toroid with windings, dimensions (OD, ID, height), and turn count. Quick presets: buck converter (47µH, 3A, 150kHz), boost converter (100µH, 2A, 100kHz), EMI filter (220µH, 1A, 50kHz), RF choke (10µH, 0.5A, 500kHz). Educational content: AL value explanation, core material properties, key formulas (L = AL×N²/1000, B_max = (L×I_max)/(N×A_e), R_dc = (ρ×l)/A_wire), design tips (keep flux <80% B_sat, use litz wire for HF, skin effect considerations, ±20% tolerance margin, temperature derating), common applications (buck/boost converters, EMI/RFI filters, RF chokes, flyback energy storage, resonant tanks), wire gauge selection guide (by current range), and winding method considerations.
